Theorem 3.2.2. A convergence criterion for the trigonometric Fourier series.

Suppose that \(g\) is a \(2\pi\) periodic function on \(\bbR\) and \(x\in (-\pi, \pi)\) is such that there exist \(\delta>0\) and \(M < \infty\) such that
\begin{equation} |g(x+t)-g(x)|\le M |t| \text{ for all } t\in (-\delta, \delta).\tag{3.2.1} \end{equation}
Then \(S_N[g]( x)-g(x)\to 0\) as \(N\to\infty\text{.}\)

Proof.

in-context
